revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:秋田諏訪宮]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = Shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Akita-Suwa-gū.jpg |Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Akita Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | location = 19 Motomichi, Rokugo, Misato Town, Senboku District, Akita Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|39.423611|N|140.542778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|1=Takeminakata}}; {{ill|Yasakatome|qid=Q9150392|1=Yasakatome}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine; Beppyo Shrine | founded = Traditional date: Enryaku 21 (802) | festival = {{ill|August 24|en|August 24|qid=Q2825|lt=August 24|1=August 24}} | map = Japan Akita | location = {{ill|Misato|qid=Q1349377|1=Misato, Akita}} }} '''Akita Suwa Shrine''' (秋田諏訪宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Misato Town]], {{ill|Senboku District|en|Senboku District|qid=Q398444|lt=Senboku District|1=Senboku District}}, {{ill|Akita Prefecture|en|Akita Prefecture|qid=Q81863|lt=Akita Prefecture|1=Akita Prefecture}}. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine and is currently listed as a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. == History == * {{ill|802|en|802|qid=Q29992|lt=802|1=802}} (Enryaku 21) - Tradition holds that the shrine was founded by [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]. * {{ill|1087|en|1087|qid=Q19461|lt=1087|1=1087}} (Kanji 1) - During the [[:en:Later Three-Year War|Later Three-Year War]], it is said that Suwa Daimyojin from {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|qid=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} worshiped at the shrine while in camp. Upon his return, he enshrined a statue of a Daiguji (chief priest) and left one son to serve as a priest, establishing the Suwa (Suwa) family as hereditary chief priests. * {{ill|1192|en|1192|qid=Q19739|lt=1192|1=1192}} (Kenkyū 3) - Records show the rebuilding of the shrine buildings. * {{ill|1385|en|1385|qid=Q6350|lt=1385|1=1385}} (Shitoku era) - [[Nikaido Michiharu]] established the shrine as the general protector of several neighboring villages<ref name="akitajiten">''Akita Encyclopedia'', Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074</ref>. Tradition holds that Suwa Munetaka from the Suwa family in Kamakura, Sagami Province, came to the shrine and became the 11th chief priest. * {{ill|1604|en|1604|qid=Q6738|lt=1604|1=1604}} (Keichō 9) - {{ill|Satake Yoshishige|en|Satake Yoshishige|qid=Q1133798|lt=Satake Yoshishige|1=Satake Yoshishige}} built new shrine buildings (surviving ridgepole inscription). * {{ill|1723|en|1723|qid=Q6921|lt=1723|1=1723}} (Kyōhō 8) - Current main hall built. * {{ill|1810|en|1810|qid=Q6922|lt=1810|1=1810}} (Bunka 7) - "Suwa Shrine" plaque inscribed by Yokote Castle governor Tomura Yoshimichi installed. * {{ill|1845|en|1845|qid=Q7636|lt=1845|1=1845}} (Kōka 2) - The chief priest became directly controlled by the Jisha-bugyō (temple and shrine magistrate). * {{ill|1869|en|1869|qid=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}} (Meiji 2) - The shrine was designated as the general protector of {{ill|Ugo Province|en|Ugo Province|qid=Q1192070|lt=Ugo Province|1=Ugo Province}}. * {{ill|1881|en|1881|qid=Q7808|lt=1881|1=1881}} (Meiji 14) - The shrine served as a rest stop during the {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|qid=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}}'s tour of the Tohoku region<ref name="akitajiten"/>. * {{ill|1919|en|1919|qid=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}} (Taisho 8) - Ranked as a prefectural shrine. * {{ill|1920|en|1920|qid=Q2155|lt=1920|1=1920}} (Taisho 9) - Current worship hall built. * {{ill|1953|en|1953|qid=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}} (Showa 28) - Listed as a Beppyo Shrine. * {{ill|1987|en|1987|qid=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}} (Showa 62) - Officially named Akita Suwa Shrine. The shrine is unique in Akita Prefecture for having maintained its status as an independent Shinto shrine, without becoming associated with Buddhist or Shugendo temples, even before the Kami-Buddha Separation Order. The Suwa family has consistently held the Rank of Shinto priest. The shrine is known for the unique [[:en:Koshogatsu|Koshogatsu]] ritual "Rokugo Kamakura festival," which includes the "Takeuchi" (Bamboo Battle) ceremony, designated as a [[National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] on January 14, 1982. == Deities == The shrine enshrines [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], [[Yasakatome no Mikoto]], among others. == festivals == * Rokugo Kamakura festival (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) ** {{ill|February 11|en|February 11|qid=Q2335|lt=February 11|1=February 11}} - Kura-biraki, Tenpen ** {{ill|February 13|en|February 13|qid=Q2333|lt=February 13|1=February 13}} - Tori-oi festival ** {{ill|February 15|en|February 15|qid=Q2334|lt=February 15|1=February 15}} - Tenpen-yaki, Takeuchi * {{ill|August 24|en|August 24|qid=Q2825|lt=August 24|1=August 24}} - festival == Access == * By bus: 20 minutes from {{ill|JR|en|JR|qid=Q127942|lt=JR|1=JR}} {{ill|Ōmagari Station (Akita)|en|Ōmagari Station (Akita)|qid=Q1052987|lt=Ōmagari Station|1=Ōmagari Station (Akita)}} on [[Ugo Kotsu]] Bus, get off at Rokugo-Ōmachi or Rokugo-Kamimachi Bus Stop, then 5-minute walk. * By taxi: 5 minutes from {{ill|JR|en|JR|qid=Q127942|lt=JR|1=JR}} {{ill|Iizume Station|en|Iizume Station|qid=Q5995055|lt=Iizume Station|1=Iizume Station}} (3.5 km). * By car: 30 minutes from [[Yokote Interchange]] on the {{ill|Akita Expressway|en|Akita Expressway|qid=Q1060262|lt=Akita Expressway|1=Akita Expressway}} (17 km), 20 minutes from [[Ōmagari Interchange]] (12 km). == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Related Articles == * [[Suwa Shrine]] * [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] * [[Tachibana Kozomu]] - Lived at the shrine during his childhood. == External Links == * [http://www.akitasuwagu.jp/ Akita Suwa Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto-stub|qq=}} == References == {{reflist|qq=}}
{{Suwa Faith|qq=}} {{Beppyo Shrines|qq=}} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} <!--This came from everybodywiki but the history is imported here. There is no longer a need for the template-->
{{translated page|ja|秋田諏訪宮|version=101986390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories|
[[simple:Akita Suwa Shrine]] }}

<!--all this actually mandates is a completion tier 7 ranking. We are not using the promising draft template here. If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever--><!--Akita Suwa Shrine--><!--Akita Suwa Shrine--><!--Akita Suwa Shrine--><!--this page is not from a simple english wikipedia draft. Categorizing it as such was an error. It is from an archived english wikipedia draft-->
